name: Green Beans Braised with Mint & Potatoes
recipeIngredient: Salt and freshly ground pepper – trimmed and cut
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the fat in an enameled pan.
2. Mix in the tomato juice or sauce.
3. Add the green beans and parsley to the pan with enough water to almost cover.
4. Tuck the potato slices in between, partially cover the pan, and simmer for 25 minutes.
5. Stir occasionally and season with salt, pepper, and 2 tablespoons chopped mint.
6. Cook uncovered until the beans and potatoes are fork tender, about 10 more minutes.
7. If the sauce has not thickened, pour it into a small pan and boil down to one cup. Combine with the beans and potatoes in a warm serving bowl.
8. Sprinkle with additional fresh mint and serve warm.
Excellent with grilled chops, fish or egg dish, but equally good as a main course with cheese.
From: "The Food of Greece" by Vilma Liacouras Chantiles. Avenel Books, New York. via Karen Mintzias
